---
title: "NV Control Framework"
page-layout: full
toc: true
---


## Modular Python framework for experimental control of diamond NV center systems

<div class="hero-buttons">

[GitHub Repository](#){.btn .btn-primary}

[Documentation](#){.btn .btn-outline-primary}

[Development Roadmap](#development-roadmap){.btn .btn-outline-primary}

</div>

---

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6 .g-col-lg-3}

### Status

🟢 Active Development

:::

::: {.g-col-6 .g-col-lg-3}

### Language

Python

:::

::: {.g-col-6 .g-col-lg-3}

### Architecture

Modular OOP

:::

::: {.g-col-6 .g-col-lg-3}

### Platform

Cross Platform

:::

:::

---

---

## Overview

The **NV Control Framework** is an object-oriented Python platform for controlling and automating experiments based on nitrogen-vacancy (NV) centers in diamond.

The framework provides a unified architecture for integrating laboratory hardware, experiment logic, graphical interfaces, and data acquisition into a scalable and maintainable software ecosystem.

---

## Why This Framework?

Modern quantum sensing experiments require the coordinated control of lasers, microwave sources, pulse generators, scientific cameras, motion stages, power supplies, and data acquisition systems.

As laboratories evolve, software often becomes tightly coupled to specific hardware, making maintenance and expansion increasingly difficult.

The NV Control Framework addresses these challenges through:

- Modular hardware abstraction
- Reusable experiment classes
- Instrument-independent APIs
- Scalable software architecture
- Automated experiment workflows
- Future-ready design

---

## Current Capabilities

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

::: {.project-card}

### Instrument Control

- Scientific cameras
- Microwave sources
- Motion stages
- Pulse generators
- Power supplies

:::

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

::: {.project-card}

### Experiment Framework

- ODMR
- Pulse sequencing
- Experiment management
- Automation
- Data acquisition

:::

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

::: {.project-card}

### Software Engineering

- Modular architecture
- Hardware abstraction
- JSON configuration
- Object-oriented design
- Git version control

:::

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

::: {.project-card}

### Future Development

- Rabi
- Ramsey
- T₁ / T₂
- Live imaging
- Quantum Machines OPX

:::

:::

:::

---

## Supported Hardware

| Instrument | Status |
|------------|:------:|
| Andor Neo Camera | ✅ |
| IDS Event Camera | ✅ |
| SG384 Microwave Source | ✅ |
| Swabian Pulse Streamer | ✅ |
| SmarAct XYZ Stages | ✅ |
| Keysight Power Supplies | ✅ |
| Quantum Machines OPX | 🚧 |

---

## Software Philosophy

The framework follows a layered architecture:

```
Graphical User Interface
            │
Experiment Manager
            │
Experiment Classes
            │
Hardware Manager
            │
Instrument Drivers
```

Each layer has a clearly defined responsibility, allowing new hardware and experiments to be added with minimal changes to the rest of the framework.

---

### Software Architecture

The NV Control Framework is organized as a modular, layered software architecture that separates user interaction, experiment logic, hardware abstraction, configuration management, and the physical experimental platform. This design enables new hardware devices and experimental protocols to be integrated without modifying the overall framework.

![](assets/architecture_v1.png){fig-alt="NV Control Framework Architecture" width=100%}

*Figure 1. Architecture of the NV Control Framework (Version 1.0).*
---

## Development Roadmap

| Version | Major Milestone | Status |
|----------|-----------------|--------|
| v0.1 | ODMR Framework | ✅ Complete |
| v0.2 | GUI Development | 🚧 In Progress |
| v0.3 | Rabi Measurements | Planned |
| v0.4 | Ramsey Measurements | Planned |
| v0.5 | T₁ / T₂ | Planned |
| v0.6 | Live Imaging | Planned |
| v0.7 | Quantum Machines OPX | Planned |
| v1.0 | Stable Release | Target |

---

## Open Source Development

The NV Control Framework is developed following modern software engineering principles including:

- Modular design
- Version control with Git
- Continuous development
- Automated testing
- Hardware abstraction
- Documentation-driven development

The long-term objective is to create a reusable software platform for quantum sensing laboratories.

---

---

## Continue Exploring

- [← Research Portfolio](../index.qmd)

- [Research](../../pages/research.qmd)

- [Software](../../pages/software.qmd)

- [Homepage](../../index.qmd)
